vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but fails to account for the rapid degradation of the specimens. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is the only stable anchor. P1 and P2 are largely obscured or in states of advanced senescence.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: Specimen is no longer visible or has withered into the substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage remains; pot appears empty or contains only debris.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos leaf and White Rabbit anchor remain.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No silver guest foliage detected.

plant_audit.P3: The Pothos leaf shows signs of chlorosis and potential edge necrosis. The rabbit remains the only constant.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all visible pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of eggshell fragments in the foreground pot, acting as a new, non-botanical structural element.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state showed a single green leaf in the foreground pot and a withered brown leaf in the background.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Foreground leaf shows progressive wilting and loss of turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Foreground leaf has effectively collapsed or been obscured by the introduction of eggshell debris; the biome is in a state of terminal decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic failure of plant life. The lack of light (implied by the dark, low-contrast images) and potential substrate issues have led to the death of the registered specimens.
anomalies: New Introduction: Eggshell fragments placed in the foreground pot.
Systemic Loss: Total disappearance of P1, P2, and P4 foliage.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has transitioned from a struggling collection to a state of near-total collapse. The foreground pot, initially hosting a single green shoot, has been repurposed or cluttered with eggshells, while the background pots are devoid of healthy vegetation. The Pothos in the rabbit pot is the sole survivor, though it appears stressed.
